published_prior_art.cite: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023SPL and BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023COLT already cover functionals identified despite nonunique nuisance bridges; finite active-face packaging is narrower.
DarollesFanFlorensRenault2011Econometrica and BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023COLT cover Tikhonov/penalty-determined nuisance limits; this is routine finite convex analysis.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy_v1 sharp joint proxy-null mediation set and eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator_v1 proxy-envelope PNDE bounds are adjacent but different targets.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The exact-fiber Tikhonov limit is a standard penalty-selector limit already covered by the inverse-problem and weak-nuisance literature; keep it as support, not a novelty claim.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The hypotheses include ATE-active bridge nonuniqueness, which forces the non-invariant regime, so the kappa_inv(P)=0 side of the claimed iff frontier is outside the stated assumptions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Split Conjecture 1 into an invariant-regime statement without Assumption active-nonunique and a non-invariant selection-range statement with it, so the iff frontier is not vacuous on one side.
Demote Theorem 2 to a setup lemma and cite Darolles et al. plus Bennett et al. as the standard source for penalty-selected nuisance limits.

Make the flagship workflow comparison sharper by writing the exact population quadratic criteria for the Cui proximal OR residual-ridge step and the finite-class Kallus-Mao-Uehara minimax specialization.
Add the closed-form Q_lambda minimizer and endpoint range for the binary witness to show explicitly that the witness is nondegenerate and moves the ATE.

recommended_next_step: Patch Conjecture 1's assumptions and reclassify Theorem 2 as standard support before sending the proposal forward.
